operating_state: Total revenue increased 3% to $25.2 billion driven by net interest income growth of $614 million to $14.4 billion from higher rates and loan expansion, partially offset by elevated funding costs. Consumer Banking grew from credit card balance increases while Global Wealth declined from deposit outflows to higher-yielding alternatives. Global Banking and Markets delivered improved performance with trading revenue gains. Deposits stabilized at $1.88 trillion despite customer migration to higher-yielding products.
cost_structure: Noninterest expense rose $535 million to $15.8 billion from technology investments, FDIC assessments, and business liquidation costs, maintaining 62.9% efficiency ratio. Credit costs increased materially with provision rising $336 million to $1.2 billion driven by card growth and quality normalization. Consumer net charge-offs rose significantly while commercial losses increased primarily from office real estate exposure.
material_events: Board increased quarterly dividend to $0.24 from $0.22 and repurchased $1.0 billion common stock with $13.6 billion authorization remaining. Federal Reserve reduced stress capital buffer to 2.5% from 3.4%, lowering CET1 minimum to 9.5% effective October 2023. The bank issued $54.0 billion long-term debt while retiring $33.0 billion during nine months.
macro_exposures: Office commercial real estate presents elevated risk with $18.1 billion outstanding and $5.1 billion criticized, facing structural demand challenges and refinancing pressures. FDIC special assessment could result in approximately $1.9 billion expense from regional bank failures. Interest rate sensitivity remains positive at $3.1 billion for 100 basis point increases despite deposit mix changes.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued rate benefits while monitoring funding cost pressures and deposit migration, maintaining expense discipline and technology investment focus. Proposed regulatory capital rules introduce expanded risk-based requirements phased over three years beginning July 2025. Strong capital position with 11.9% CET1 ratio supports lending growth and returns.
uncertainty_profile: Credit normalization continues with consumer metrics rising from historic lows, particularly cards remaining below 2019 delinquency levels. Office real estate requires monitoring with $8.7 billion maturing by end-2024 amid uncertain space demand. Pending capital rule changes could materially impact requirements while geopolitical tensions create ongoing credit and trading revenue uncertainty.
